summary.intent: Update the Moltis website to better showcase its provider/LLM support with visual pills (inspired by Polyphony), add competitive positioning content, and bring the homepage up to date with the product's current feature set.
summary.outcome: Homepage rebuilt with a provider pills section (4 rows, 29+ providers with brand icons), positioning paragraph, 3-step quickstart, 8 use-case cards, community quote with Discord source links, updated LoC stats, CSS extracted to style.css, all 9 locale files updated with translations, and all changes pushed to PR #567.

repo: i18n is handled by website/scripts/build-i18n.mjs which generates locale files from index.en.html via string substitution — French (index.fr.html) was never added to this script and was maintained manually, causing it to be missed during regeneration.
Locale files must be regenerated explicitly via `node scripts/build-i18n.mjs` after English source changes; they do not auto-update.

The build script has both top-level `title`/`metaDesc` properties per language AND `strings` array pairs — both must be updated when English copy changes, or old translations persist in the generated files.
Brand icons are best sourced from Simple Icons (simpleicons.org) for SVG; raster logos from official sites need separate handling (grayscale filter, no dark-mode invert).
website/images/icons/ did not exist before this session — it was created to hold extracted SVG/raster icon assets.

code.finding: Each language entry has both a `title`/`metaDesc` field (used for <title> and OG tags) and a `strings` array of [English, translated] pairs for body content. Changing English copy requires updating both independently.

Newly created by extracting all inline <style> blocks from index.en.html. Added `.pill-icon` (SVG, dark:invert) and `.pill-icon-color` (raster logos, grayscale filter, no invert) classes for dual dark-mode strategies.

workflow: Playwright MCP was used for visual verification of the dev server — effective for catching rendering issues (broken icons, dark mode) without manual browser checks.
Background Bash tasks for the dev server created noise via task-notification messages that required handling between interactions.
When updating copy across locale files, sed on all index.*.html files is faster than editing each locale individually.
SVGs using `currentColor` for fill work well with CSS `filter: invert(1)` for dark mode, but raster (webp/png) logos need `grayscale(1)` instead of invert to avoid color artifacts.

friction: French locale was silently excluded from build-i18n.mjs — not discovered until the user tested http://localhost:4000/index.fr.html and saw English content.
LoC stat updates and then all homepage changes were initially applied only to index.en.html, requiring a second pass to propagate to all locales after user pointed it out.
Multiple background dev server task-notifications required reading output files mid-conversation, breaking flow.
Teams and DeepSeek icons were broken on first attempt — Teams used a crude 'T' glyph, DeepSeek used an incomplete heart shape — requiring replacement iterations.
MiniMax icon fetched from official URL included the full wordmark with text, requiring ImageMagick cropping to extract just the waveform icon.
Cerebras icon went through multiple iterations (first attempt had white background issues, second attempt still felt broken to user).
LM Studio has no public SVG, forcing use of a .webp app icon with special CSS handling.
open_items: Machine-translated locale content (de, es, fr, it, ja, ko, pt, ru, zh) for new sections (positioning, how-it-works, use cases, community quote) should be reviewed by native speakers.
French was added to build-i18n.mjs near end of session but the final state of whether French translations are in the script vs. hardcoded in HTML is unclear — needs verification.
MiniMax, Z.AI, and Venice pills have no official brand icons; only text labels.
LM Studio pill uses a .webp raster icon instead of SVG — would benefit from an official SVG if one becomes available.

New homepage sections (positioning, how-it-works, use cases) exist only in English in index.en.html — the build-i18n.mjs string pairs for these sections were not added, so regenerating locales via the build script would not translate them.
PR #567 was created but not reviewed or merged.

Other locale files (non-English) for the website were not updated as part of the icons extraction to website/images/icons/ — they still reference icons via relative paths that should work, but this should be verified.
